page: Amazon Web Services r5d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It features 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most memory and database workloads. The server achieves average ratings in memory bandwidth tests, Passmark memory scores, and Redis SET operations, which reached 6.82 million operations per second. However, single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the weak category. LLM inference speeds are also weak to poor, particularly for medium-sized models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5d.2xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio combined with fast local NVMe SSD storage, making it suitable for memory-intensive applications that require temporary local storage. The tradeoff lies in its compute performance; while memory capacity and bandwidth are solid, the older Intel Xeon processor architecture delivers lower single-threaded and multi-threaded CPU throughput compared to compute-optimized alternatives.

This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, including medium-sized databases, caching layers like Redis, data buffering, and real-time analytics. The inclusion of 300 GB local NVMe SSD storage also makes it appropriate for applications requiring high-speed scratch space or temporary database tables.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring high-speed local storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, it features 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance includes 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average performance in memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and database tasks, though CPU-heavy benchmarks and LLM inference speeds are relatively weak. This hardware profile makes the r5d.2xlarge highly suitable for database operations, caching layers, and applications requiring temporary high-speed scratch space, offering a balanced tradeoff between high memory capacity, local SSD storage, and moderate compute capabilities.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 300 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database workloads and caching applications.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 300 GB local NVMe SSD instance storage
Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz
Average performance in Redis and database operations
Best suited for databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
